articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

articles.description: Debra Matabvu Herald Reporter GOVERNMENT has identified at least eight mining sites — mainly in the lithium and iron sectors — for designation as Special Economic Zones, in a major... The post Beneficiation: Govt takes lead appeared first on herald .

articles.title: Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush
articles.description: Rutendo Nyeve-Bulawayo Bureau A RECORD number of local and international tourists are visiting the Victoria Falls as the waterfall reaches its peak, defying fears that numbers could drop due to... The post Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush appeared first on herald .

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zim should seek new tobacco markets to absorb output surge
more.description: Source: Zim should seek new tobacco markets to absorb output surge – herald Tapiwanashe Mangwiro Zimbabwe needs to aggressively seek new tobacco export markets to maintain firm domestic prices, as traditional buyers can no longer absorb the country’s high output volumes, the Parliamentary Portfolio Committee on Lands, Agriculture, Fisheries, Water and Rural Development has said. [...] The post Zim should seek new tobacco markets to absorb output surge appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Enact law to protect national infrastructure, Government told
more.description: Source: Enact law to protect national infrastructure, Government told – herald Nyore Madzianike Senior Reporter The Government must urgently consider the enactment of legislation that specifically addresses the protection of critical national infrastructure in all sectors of the economy, which are increasingly exposed to both State-sponsored and non-State cyber threats. Speaker of Parliament Advocate Jacob [...] The post Enact law to protect national infrastructure, Government told appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ‘Companies absorb Middle East war cost drivers, keep prices steady’
more.description: Source: ‘Companies absorb Middle East war cost drivers, keep prices steady’ – herald Martin Kadzere Industry and Commerce Minister Mangaliso Ndlovu has said the prices of most basic commodities in Zimbabwe have remained relatively stable despite cost pressures from surging fuel prices triggered by the ongoing Middle East crisis. Speaking at a dialogue on geopolitical [...] The post ‘Companies absorb Middle East war cost drivers, keep prices steady’ appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: New ZiG banknotes improve liquidity, market stability
more.description: Source: New ZiG banknotes improve liquidity, market stability – herald Moffat Mungazi Business Reporter THE injection of upgraded BiG5 ZiG banknotes into circulation by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) earlier this week has significantly improved liquidity, with the market maintaining stability and avoiding sharp price increases for basic commodities and spike in the parallel [...] The post New ZiG banknotes improve liquidity, market stability appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ZFU and Empower Bank partner to drive financial inclusion for farmers
more.description: Source: ZFU and Empower Bank partner to drive financial inclusion for farmers – herald Elita Chikwati Agriculture News Editor The Zimbabwe Farmers union has signed a Memorandum of Agreement with Empower Bank to boost funding access for smallholder farmers, with a focus on women and youth in rural areas. Under the agreement, ZFU will act as an [...] The post ZFU and Empower Bank partner to drive financial inclusion for farmers appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Govt slashes agric sector levies, licence fees
more.description: Source: Govt slashes agric sector levies, licence fees – herald Herald Reporter THE Government has reviewed licences, permits, levies and fees in the agriculture sector, slashing charges for fertiliser producers and horticulture exporters, reducing the cotton buying point levy by 75 percent, and scrapping fish harvest fees, among other measures. This comes after Cabinet recently [...] The post Govt slashes agric sector levies, licence fees appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: VP Chiwenga to light Independence Flame in Beitbridge
more.description: Source: VP Chiwenga to light Independence Flame in Beitbridge – herald Thupeyo Muleya Beitbridge Bureau VICE President Constantino Chiwenga is expected to arrive in Beitbridge this morning, where he will officiate at the lighting of the Independence Flame at Juliet (Zezani) Assembly Point before a large crowd of community members drawn from across Matabeleland South [...] The post VP Chiwenga to light Independence Flame in Beitbridge appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Between Principle and Pragmatism: The Moral Fault Lines in Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Debate
more.description: Strip away the procedural arguments, the partisan loyalties, and the ritualised exchanges of constitutional interpretation, and the Zimbabwean debate over amendment reveals something older and more enduring than law. Beneath the legal vocabulary lies a contest between competing moral imaginations, different ways of understanding authority, time, obligation, and political possibility.Zimbabwe travel guide By Brighton Musonza […]The post Between Principle and Pragmatism: The Moral Fault Lines in Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Debate appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: World Bank downgrades sub-Saharan Africa growth projection amid geopolitical risks
more.description: The World Bank on Wednesday revised its 2026 economic growth projection for sub-Saharan Africa downward by 0.3 percentage points from the October 2025 forecast, citing geopolitical risks. According to the latest edition of the Africa Economic Update, sub-Saharan Africa’s economic recovery from a decade of global shocks is showing signs of stalling. While growth is […]

more.title: Rights body witnessed intimidation, whip-wielding vetters at CAB3 hearings
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s human rights watchdog has raised the alarm over widespread intimidation and harassment of opponents of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 during parliamentary public hearings, including the use of men armed with whips to vet participants in Mashonaland West. The Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) deployed monitoring teams across all provinces from March […]

more.title: Insisting on a referendum devoid of a legal basis, says AG
more.description: Attorney-General Mrs Virginia Mabiza has dismissed calls for a referendum on the proposed constitutional changes, saying the demands have no legal basis and are driven by political considerations rather than constitutional requirements. In an interview, Mrs Mabiza said the Constitution clearly defines the limited circumstances under which a referendum is required, and argued that the […]

more.title: Calls Grow for Referendum as Constitutional Amendment Bill Hearings Turn Contentious
more.description: Public hearings on Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 have entered a critical phase, with growing calls from citizens and analysts for the proposed changes to be subjected to a national referendum. The Bill, currently before the Parliament of Zimbabwe, seeks to extend the terms of office for councillors, Members of Parliament and the President […]

more.title: Chinamasa under fire over ‘colonial sins’ remarks after Coltart assault
more.description: Patrick Chinamasa has ignited controversy after suggesting that human rights lawyer Douglas Coltart was made to “atone” for the alleged colonial legacy of his father following a violent incident in Harare. The remarks come after Coltart was reportedly assaulted by suspected ZANU-PF supporters during a public hearing for Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 at the […]
